steps.title: Use the square fact
steps.content: For any real value of $x$, the quantity $(x-1)^2$ cannot be negative. Its smallest possible value is $0$, when $x=1$.

steps.title: Compare to the equation
steps.content: The equation asks for $(x-1)^2=-4$, but $-4$ is below the minimum possible value of the left side. On a graph, $y=(x-1)^2$ never intersects $y=-4$.

steps.title: Count the real solutions
steps.content: Since there is no intersection and no real square can equal $-4$, the equation has zero distinct real solutions. Choice D is correct.
